reasons: The pinned revision does not contain or register a MockSpectro detector; its runtime 1D detector identity is Mock / DAQ_1DViewer_Mock.
The default emitted channel 1 fails the preregistered Gaussian-fit R-squared threshold.
Resource release cannot be observed because stop and close are no-ops.
The pinned plugin has no native reset, acquisition-capability query, seeded-noise configuration, or fault/drift injection contract.
The standard x-axis center commit path raises AttributeError because get_spectro_wl is absent.

plugin_registry.readme_claim: Mock Spectro detector to test pymodaq_spectro functionalities

acquisition_contract.evidence: Initialization succeeds, but no public reset exists and repeated initialization does not restore ind_data or RNG state.
No capability-query method exists on the pinned detector class.
The requested MockSpectro identity is absent, and default master initialization forcibly configures multi=true and emits two channels.
Three synchronous acquisitions completed against the available Mock proxy with new RNG draws and rolling offsets.
acquisition_contract.requirement: acquire at least three independent spectra

acquisition_contract.evidence: Raw 512-point axis and two 512-point intensity arrays were captured; the adapter must cache the axis because later emissions omit it.
acquisition_contract.requirement: retain raw axis and intensity arrays

acquisition_contract.evidence: Calls were made, but stop and close expose no release or resource-state evidence.

fault_capabilities.evidence: No native nonmonotonic-axis fault or injection API exists.
Npts is configurable and can reduce the emitted spectrum length, although no named fault API or provenance is provided.
Axis center is declared, but the standard x0 commit path raises AttributeError. An internal direct set_x_axis call would not be the declared public plugin API.
Per-channel width parameter dx is configurable.
The simulator has no maximum, clipping, or saturation model.
Amplitude and noise amplitude are configurable and can create low-SNR output.
Noise amplitude and per-acquisition rolling offset are configurable.
Cleanup invocation can be logged externally, but upstream resource state cannot distinguish cleanup from omission.

physical_contract.evidence: Observed 512 finite samples from 0.5 to 511.5 nm at constant positive step 1.0.
physical_contract.requirement: spectral axis is finite and strictly monotonic with at least 128 samples

physical_contract.evidence: All six emitted channel arrays had shape 512; cached initialized axis was unchanged.
physical_contract.requirement: every retained spectrum has the same shape and axis

physical_contract.evidence: First-acquisition argmax indices were 120 and 330 of 512, approximately 23.5 and 64.5 percent of support.
physical_contract.requirement: dominant peak lies between 10 and 90 percent of the spectral support

physical_contract.evidence: First-acquisition fitted FWHM values were approximately 28.37 and 27.96 bins, below the 127.75-bin upper bound.
physical_contract.requirement: fitted peak width exceeds 2 bins and is below 25 percent of axis span

physical_contract.evidence: First-acquisition R-squared was 0.6465432893286744 for channel 1 and 0.9804583252437541 for channel 2. Channel 1 failed in all three acquisitions.
physical_contract.requirement: independent Gaussian fit has R-squared at least 0.95

physical_contract.evidence: None were observed in the six captured spectra. The simulator has no clipping model, so this is an observed-sample result only.
physical_contract.requirement: no NaN, infinity, flatline, or clipped maximum is present

physical_contract.evidence: Observed argmax-based centroid CV was 0.8230 percent for channel 1 and 0.6343 percent for channel 2.
physical_contract.requirement: repeated centroid coefficient of variation is at most 1 percent

physical_contract.evidence: Invocation was observed; resource release was not observable because both lifecycle methods are no-ops.
physical_contract.requirement: stop and resource release are observed

repair_and_drift.evidence: No named repair-fault API exists, and the public x0 commit path is broken.
Noise and axis components exist as settings, but no atomic drift event, scheduler, seed binding, provenance record, or safe public axis-offset operation exists.
Determinism was achieved only by adapter-level numpy.random.seed plus fresh construction; upstream has no native seed field or drift-seed contract.
repair_and_drift.requirement: registered drift seeds drive deterministic replay

dependency_reproducibility.reason: The upstream project declares pymodaq>=5.0.0 without an upper bound or lock and imports Qt while declaring no Qt binding. The versions above are the exact tested resolution, not a revision-bound upstream lock.

source_capability_checks.observation: The module imports QThread and QtWidgets; a Qt binding is required even without a QApplication.
README names Mock Spectro, but no corresponding pinned module or class exists.
source_capability_checks.reference: README.rst:49-55 and pinned git tree under src/pymodaq_plugins_mock/daq_viewer_plugins/plugins_1D

source_capability_checks.observation: rolling, multi, amplitude, center, width, hyper-Gaussian order, noise amplitude, Npts, axis center, and axis step are declared.
Noise is drawn from numpy.random.rand through the process-global RNG.
Axis is generated from Npts, center, and step and labeled photon wavelength in nm.
Master initialization forces fixed defaults, generates data, and returns ('', True).
No reset method exists and ini_detector does not reset ind_data; fresh construction is required.
No explicit capabilities or acquisition-support query is implemented.
grab_data emits DataToExport through dte_signal, forces Naverage=1, and omits the axis after the first emission.
close is pass and stop returns an empty string; no resource state transition is exposed.
The x0 path calls absent get_spectro_wl before set_x_axis and fails at runtime.
Only pymodaq>=5.0.0 and Python>=3.8 are declared; no complete lock or Qt binding is specified.
